Fern Por-rrrcAl Pnecrrcss CoMMrssroN
428 J Street o Suire 620 o Sacramento, CA 95g14_2329
t9l6) 322-5660 o Fax (9t6) 322_0886
October 3, 2008
Steven Lavagnino
908 Speed Street
Santa Maria, CA93454
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-08-156
Dear Mr. Lavagnino:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conlict-of-
interest provisions of the political Reform Act (the ,'Act,,)., E;.;;r"" y;ffi"rtio, ao",
not relate to a specific governmental decision, we consider your request to be a request
for informal assistance-' o.,. assistance is limited to provisions of the Act and does not
address areas of the law outside the Act, such as Government Code Section 1090.
QUESTION
Does a conflict of interest exist if you are elected as a county supervisor at the
same time your father serves as a city's mayor within that supervisorial district?
CONCLUSION
There are no provisions in the Act that prohibit you from seeking a seat on the
county Board of SuPervisors while your fatheiserves u, Muyo. of Santa Maria, which is
wholly contained within that supervisorial district. Holding u pori,i* on'it. srr,"
Barbara County Board of Supervisors does not, in and of itielf, create a potenti;lly
disqualifying conflict of interest for you under the Act.
However, if you are elected to the County Board of Supervisors, you would need
to disqualify yourself from making, participatinj in making, or influencing any
I The Political Reform Act is contained in Government code Sections g1000 through gl0l4. All
statutory references are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulatio,i, nr tn. p,oi.
PoliticalPractices Commission are contained in Sections lsll0 througi tgssl of ritle 2 of the Califbrnia
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2. Division-6 of the Califbrnia Code of
Regulatittns. unless otherwise indiqated.
r Infbrmal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an ,pinion or
tbrmal wrimen advice (Regulation ltt329(c) enclosed.)


File No. 08-
Page No.2
govemmental decision that would have a reasonably foreseeable and material financial
effect on one or more of your economic interests, as generally described below and in the
enclosed materials.
FACTS
Your father is the mayor of the City of Santa Maria. You have announced your
candidacy for the Santa Barbara Board of Supervisors, Fifth District. The election will be
held in 2010. An anticipated opponent has expressed concem that, if elected, you may
have a conflict of interest because the city of Santa Maria is wholly within the boundaries
of the Fifth district.
Ai\ALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest decisions do not prevent an individual from running
for, or holding, elective office or from being employed by a public agency. Instead, the
Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officiils witt pirfoim their duties
in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financiaiinterests or the
financial interests of persons who have supported them. (Section gl00l(b).)
Specifically, Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in
making or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a [ouer.rmertal
decision in which the official has a financial interest.
You have not provided information, and do not ask for advice, on a specific
governmental decision or the decision's effect on your economic interests. We enclose a
booklet entitled "Can I Vote?" which gives an overview of the Act's conflict-of-interest
provisions. This may assist you in determining whether you have a conflict-of-interest
issue in the future, on which we would be pleased to offer assistance.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (gt6) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Amber Maltbie
Legal Intern, Legal Division
